pattern: Ending the j-th chosen subarray at some position only ever needs the best total achievable from a start point at least m earlier -- carrying that running best forward turns an O(n^2) subarray-partition search into a single O(n*k) sweep.
hints: With prefix sums, the value of a subarray between a start and an end is one subtraction, so 'place a subarray of length at least m ending here' becomes 'pick the best qualifying start seen so far and subtract its prefix sum'.

Build the answer one subarray at a time: for each count of subarrays used, track the best total achievable using a prefix of the array up to each position, letting a new subarray begin anywhere that leaves it at least m long.

As the ending position slides forward by one, exactly one new starting position becomes eligible -- keep a running maximum of (best total from the previous count, up to that start, minus the prefix sum at that start) so every position is handled in O(1) rather than rescanning every earlier start.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two subarrays with a slack length
statement example, all values negative and every subarray forced to length one
minimum m, single subarray: the best answer is just one element
maximum m, two disjoint length-3 subarrays needed back to back
k equal to the array length with m=1, every element is forced to be its own subarray regardless of sign
all-positive values with m as only a minimum: a solution that treats m as an exact length would stop short of the better, longer subarray
alternating signs with two subarrays of the minimum allowed length, several equally-good placements exist
all-negative values with a length-at-least-three requirement, the best choice is the least-negative qualifying run

stress.description: random array across the full value range, with m and k chosen to always leave a feasible number of subarrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    m = min(rng.randint(1, 3), n)
    max_k = n // m
    k = rng.randint(1, max_k)
    return [nums, k, m]
